items.title: The covert U.S.-China battle to make chatbots leak their secrets - The Washington Post
items.summary: In 2024, OpenAI released a tool to make it easier for customers to distill its own models and produc
items.content:
In 2024, OpenAI released a tool to make it easier for customers to distill its own models and produce data sets for AI training. SpaceX founder Elon Musk said in court testimony in May that his AI company xAI used distillation to train its models and that the technique is common throughout the industry.

Without knowing the details about how a Chinese model was trained, it can be difficult to distinguish illicit behavior from a terms of service violation, said Irene Solaiman, the chief policy officer for Hugging Face, a repository for open source AI.

# FMCG giants give consumer products an AI makeover

By   Alexander Marrow     

Jul 6 2026 9:58PM

## Nestle, Haleon, L'Oreal, Mondelez all embrace the technology.

French cosmetics company L'Oreal has used AI ⁠to identify ⁠molecules in its skincare products that can be repurposed for use in shampoo and can now create products four times faster than before, a senior executive told Reuters. [...] Consumer companies, including Nescafe owner Nestle, Sensodyne toothpaste ‌maker Haleon and chocolate maker Mondelez, are using AI ‌in ‌product innovation, helping them in some cases test ‌ingredients faster, generate recipe ideas and address supply chain ⁠vulnerabilities, executives said.

The push to integrate AI into product development comes as consumer goods companies face pressure to innovate faster and cut costs amid shifting consumer tastes.

 L'Oreal, which started using AI in its labs four ​years ago, has identified new molecules for beauty products by predicting the effect they will have on skin and hair, said ⁠Fabrice Megarbane, president of its consumer products unit. [...] L'Oreal's recent innovation was repurposing molecules used in skincare products for a shampoo that uses collagen to add lift and fullness to hair, Megarbane said.

"You can really go much faster by imagining ... new associations of molecules and new benefits of molecules," Megarbane said at the Consumer Goods Forum's Global Summit in Vienna in late June.

L'Oreal CEO Nicolas Hieronimus launched a "beauty stimulus plan" last year to spur innovation ​after L'Oreal posted its slowest group sales ⁠growth in years.

Human product innovation augmented ⁠by AI is a "game-changer" at cho

items.title: LiuGong delivers largest mining excavator yet to Jiangxi Copper - International Mining
items.summary: As the new flagship product of LiuGong’s ultra-large excavator product line, the successful delivery
items.content:
As the new flagship product of LiuGong’s ultra-large excavator product line, the successful delivery of the 9200F the OEM says holds strategic value beyond that of a single piece of equipment. “It further completes the complete equipment puzzle for LiuGong’s large-scale mines, forming efficient synergy with LiuGong’s large-tonnage wide-body vehicles, bulldozers, graders, and other products, providing large mine customers with an integrated full-process solution from mining and transportation to levelling. This is a key move for Liugong’s deep focus in the mining sector.” [...] The equipment adopts a heavy-duty structural component design, with cast steel supports for large and small arms. The platform chassis uses a reinforced design, with local welded reinforcing ribs, significantly improving the equipment’s service life and reliability. In addition, the long-life hydraulic oil equipped with it offers excellent viscosity-temperature performance, ensuring stable operation of the equipment in cold and high-temperature environments.

# News

# LiuGong delivers largest mining excavator yet to Jiangxi Copper

Posted on 6 Jul 2026

Chinese OEMs are moving up the size classes in hydraulic mining excavators. On July 6, the LiuGong 200 t class 9200F ultra-large excavator headed to frontline open-pit mining areas after successful delivery to Jiangxi Copper Group, marking it says a further breakthrough for domestically made ultra-large excavators in high-end metallic mining scenarios.

AI & Machine Learning [...] Then it moves into production. To score a transaction at the moment of authorization, each request makes a round trip between the bank’s data center and a cloud-hosted model several regions away, often over the public internet. In isolation, that path adds only tens of milliseconds. Under production load, tail latency balloons – and the authorization window is fixed. When the score arrives too late, the bank has two options: hold the customer’s transaction and degrade the experience, or approve it without the model and forfeit the protection it just spent 18 months building. The model delivers. The path to it doesn’t.

items.title: Station F ramps up as a launchpad for Europe’s hottest AI startups - TechCrunch
items.summary: The first cohort of F/ai’s program was backed by a long list of significant tech companies — AMD, An
items.content:
The first cohort of F/ai’s program was backed by a long list of significant tech companies — AMD, Anthropic, AWS, Clay, Google, G42, Hugging Face, Lovable, Meta, Microsoft, Mistral AI, OpenAI, OVHcloud, Snowflake, and Qualcomm — not to mention several VC funds.

The second cohort will add a few more big names, TechCrunch has learned: Eleven Labs, Nebius, Rippling, OpenRouter, HubSpot, and GitHub.

“The goal was to bring together all the major players and make it much easier for [AI] startups looking to launch in Europe to connect with them,” Varza said.

Two teams from the accelerator’s first batch have already gained international recognition: Alpic, which won the global grand finale of The Pitch, a competition organized by Deel; and Rippletide, which won the OpenAI Codex Hackathon. [...] + Tim Fernholz

items.title: Vision transformer framework for host based cryptojacking malware detection - Nature
items.summary: As cryptojacking malware continues to increase in sophistication, traditional detection techniques a
items.content:
As cryptojacking malware continues to increase in sophistication, traditional detection techniques are proving increasingly inadequate. This evolution necessitates the adoption of advanced methodologies, particularly those grounded in Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Deep Learning (DL), to effectively identify and counter such threats12."). AI and DL-based systems offer significant advantages in processing large-scale data, uncovering latent patterns, and detecting subtle anomalies indicative of cryptojacking activity. Unlike static, rule-based systems, these models possess adaptive learning capabilities that allow them to evolve in response to emerging malware variants, thereby improving detection accuracy and minimizing false positives over time13."). This adaptability is especially [...] Several critical research gaps persist:

 Existing IDSs frequently fail to detect stealthy and resource-draining cryptojacking malware, especially those operating at the host level.
 Many deep learning-based IDSs depend on handcrafted features or CNNs, limiting their adaptability and robustness against obfuscated malware.
 The integration of ViT architectures into IDS frameworks remains largely unexplored in the context of image-transformed binary malware detection.
 There is a lack of real-time, scalable IDS solutions that utilize end-to-end image-based learning pipelines for detecting host-based threats. [...] Historically, Convolutional Neural Networks (CNNs) have been at the core of many deep learning-based IDSs for malware classification. While CNNs demonstrate strong performance in various tasks, they often face limitations in modeling the intricate spatial relationships and subtle behavioral signatures characteristic of host-based cryptojacking attacks. These patterns, manifesting through anomalies in system calls, CPU usage, or network activity, can elude CNNs due to their limited receptive field and inductive biases15."),16."). In contrast, the emergence of Vision Transf

items.title: Microseismic monitoring with the quake neural operator - Nature
items.summary: Recently, an increasing number of seismic monitoring and earthquake catalog construction workflows h
items.content: Recently, an increasing number of seismic monitoring and earthquake catalog construction workflows have been proposed7."),26. Seismol. Res. Lett. 93, 413–425 (2022)."),27."). Moving from single-task to multi-task learning, deep neural networks have demonstrated the potential to integrate multiple processing steps into a unified AI framework for seismic monitoring28."),29."),30."). Other studies have developed customized seismic monitoring workflows by incorporating innovative AI techniques. For example, Tan et al.31.") proposed an earthquake catalog construction workflow that detects events using waveform-based source imaging32.") and AI-based semantic segmentation of 3D source images, followed by phase picking and source location. Alternatively, approaches that combine phase picking for [...] Accurate monitoring of small-scale seismic events is essential for seismological studies. Traditional techniques rely on manual or automated seismic phase picking, which often leads to inaccuracies in microseismic monitoring due to unclear phase onsets. Here we introduce the Quake Neural Operator (QNO), a deep learning algorithm that builds earthquake catalogs directly from continuous data without explicit phase picking. As a multi-task operator, QNO utilizes classification and regression to detect and locate events across arbitrary seismic network geometries. We show that QNO successfully characterizes events where state-of-the-art phase picking fails. Applying QNO to the Geysers geothermal field, we identify nearly an order of magnitude more seismic detections than reported in routine [...] The advancement of deep learning has created opportunities to automate and enhance seismic monitoring7."),8."),9."),10."). Building on the sequential workflow of seismic monitoring, deep learning techniques have been used to replace steps in this procedure11."),12."),13."),14."),15."),16."),17."),18."),19."). As an example of a deep learning-based phase picker, EQTransformer achieved more

items.title: Guiding generative models to uncover diverse and novel crystals via reinforcement learning - Nature
items.summary:
## Results

### RL framework for exploring materials space

Effective computational materials discov
items.content:
## Results

### RL framework for exploring materials space

Effective computational materials discovery strategies must not just produce plausible structures absent from databases but systematically access underexplored regions of materials space. Current generative AI models excel at interpolative novelty to create plausible structures that statistically resemble the training data. However, they face issues when extrapolating towards unfamiliar chemistries characterized by rare element combinations, unconventional coordination environments or low-probability structural motifs. To address this challenge, we propose an RL framework that guides latent denoising diffusion models, as illustrated in Fig. 1a. [...] Discovering functional crystalline materials entails navigating an immense combinatorial design space. Although recent advances in generative artificial intelligence have enabled the sampling of chemically plausible compositions and structures, a fundamental challenge remains: the objective misalignment between the likelihood-based sampling in generative modelling and the targeted focus on underexplored regions where novel compounds reside. Here we introduce a reinforcement learning framework that guides latent denoising diffusion models in finding diverse and novel, yet thermodynamically viable, crystalline compounds. Our approach integrates group-relative policy optimization with verifiable, multi-objective rewards that jointly balance creativity, stability and diversity. Beyond de novo [...] framework, which aligned a text encoder with textual descriptions via contrastive learning). By enabling property-guided control without relying on heuristic mechanisms, such as classifier-free guidance (CFG)29."), our RL framework provides a principled pathway towards more controllable generation. Furthermore, the modular design of the frameworks allows seamless alignment with diverse reward signals, thus establishing a versatile foundation for the AI-guided inverse des

items.title: Sysdig Documents First Agentic Ransomware Operation - Let's Data Science
items.summary:
## Scoring Rationale

This is a notable security development for AI/ML practitioners because it is t
items.content:
## Scoring Rationale

This is a notable security development for AI/ML practitioners because it is the first well-documented instance where an LLM-driven agent reportedly executed an end-to-end extortion chain, showing practical automation of attacker workflows while still relying on known vulnerabilities.

MoreAgentic AI news→

## Sources

Public references used for this report.

8 sources

01sysdig.comJADEPUFFER: Agentic ransomware for automated database extortion02cyberscoop.comSysdig clocks first documented case of agentic ransomware03csoonline.comThis AI agent autonomously hacked a network, adapted on the fly ...

View 5 more sources [...] ### Editorial analysis

For practitioners, JADEPUFFER composes a clear, evidence-backed case study of an LLM agent executing an entire extortion chain, not merely assisting humans. This matters because it shows automation can combine reconnaissance, credential theft, lateral movement, persistence, and extortion with rapid closed-loop retries, raising detection and incident-response challenges distinct from conventional scripted ransomware. [...] For practitioners, the incident crystallizes how LLM-driven agents can automate complex intrusion chains and compress attacker skill requirements, increasing urgency around credential hygiene, runtime controls, and detection of natural-language artifacts. According to the Sysdig Threat Research Team, researchers captured what they assess to be the first documented end-to-end agentic ransomware operation, dubbed "JADEPUFFER". Sysdig reports JADEPUFFER gained initial access by exploiting CVE-2025-3248 in an internet-facing Langflow instance, harvested credentials, pivoted to a production MySQL/Nacos server, and encrypted 1,342 Nacos configuration records while running more than 600 distinct payloads (Sysdig blog; CyberScoop; CSO Online). Michael Clark, director of threat research at Sysdig,

items.title: Physics-informed deep learning-based adaptive beamforming for phased array weather radar - Nature
items.summary: This study introduces a beamforming framework based on physics-informed deep-learning for phased arr
items.content: This study introduces a beamforming framework based on physics-informed deep-learning for phased array weather radar systems, with particular emphasis on configurations involving a large number of antenna elements. The proposed approach is designed to achieve both high computational efficiency and reliable weather target detection. Physical constraints governing array behavior are explicitly embedded into the learning process by means of a tailored loss function that minimizes the mismatch between the predicted array output and a predefined reference response. To assess the effectiveness of the physics-informed deep neural network (PIDNN) beamformer, its performance is evaluated against two classical neural network architectures commonly used in the computer science domain and also [...] In contrast, deep learning–based approaches can model complex nonlinear relationships more effectively20."). For instance, a deep-learning neural network was developed in21.") to perform an adaptive DBF; however, it was trained using true CP weights, which poses limitations since CP performs poorly when only a few pulses are available, such as 16 pulses. Convolutional neural networks (CNNs), a specialized form of deep architectures, have demonstrated strong capabilities in capturing spatial patterns and learning high-dimensional representations from complex data22."),23."),24."),25."), and have attracted significant attention in radar meteorology26, 660–664 (2020)."),27, Beijing, China, 288–295 (2019)."). In28, 1565–1575 (2023)."), a CNN was successfully applied as fast adaptive [...] commonly used in the computer science domain and also represent two different learning-based alternatives: a shallow radial basis function neural network (RBFNN) and a deeper convolutional neural network (CNN). Simulation results demonstrate that the PIDNN consistently outperforms these models in terms of beamforming accuracy and computational time. Furthermore, the practical applicability of the propo

items.summary: The advancement of deep learning has created opportunities to automate and enhance seismic monitorin
items.content: The advancement of deep learning has created opportunities to automate and enhance seismic monitoring7."),8."),9."),10."). Building on the sequential workflow of seismic monitoring, deep learning techniques have been used to replace steps in this procedure11."),12."),13."),14."),15."),16."),17."),18."),19."). As an example of a deep learning-based phase picker, EQTransformer achieved more than a twofold increase in the number of detected events over the same time period, using only 18 stations, compared to a traditional catalog built from manually picked phases using 57 stations13."). In addition to enhanced detection capability and efficiency, deep learning models also achieve arrival-time precision comparable to that of human analysts. Specifically, the mean absolute errors (MAEs) in P- [...] Accurate monitoring of small-scale seismic events is essential for seismological studies. Traditional techniques rely on manual or automated seismic phase picking, which often leads to inaccuracies in microseismic monitoring due to unclear phase onsets. Here we introduce the Quake Neural Operator (QNO), a deep learning algorithm that builds earthquake catalogs directly from continuous data without explicit phase picking. As a multi-task operator, QNO utilizes classification and regression to detect and locate events across arbitrary seismic network geometries. We show that QNO successfully characterizes events where state-of-the-art phase picking fails. Applying QNO to the Geysers geothermal field, we identify nearly an order of magnitude more seismic detections than reported in routine [...] In addition to location approaches based on picked arrival times, seismic waveforms of detected events have been directly mapped to earthquake locations using deep learning techniques8."),44."),45."),46."),47."),48."). For instance, Zhang et al.49.") applied a convolutional neural network to locate induced earthquakes in Oklahoma using a fixed seismic network. In contrast, flexibility wi

items.title: Optimized image preprocessing strategies for enhanced neural network-based defect detection in industrial automation - Nature
items.summary: Deep transfer learning approaches have shown remarkable success in various industrial domains. Resea
items.content:
Deep transfer learning approaches have shown remarkable success in various industrial domains. Research has demonstrated that R-CNN models combined with AlexNet achieve superior defect detection accuracy18, 1645. 
                  
                  
                 (2025)."), particularly when enhanced with transfer learning methodologies. Transfer learning enables models to adapt from source domains to target applications, requiring only limited new samples instead of extensive retraining. Studies have shown that transfer learning improves welding defect detection accuracy by using transferred weights, which increases the variance and contrast of the feature map. [...] ### Deep learning for industrial defect detection

Convolutional Neural Networks have emerged as the dominant approach for industrial defect detection due to their hierarchical feature learning capabilities and spatial pattern recognition. Recent advances demonstrate that CNN-based systems can achieve exceptional performance when combined with appropriate preprocessing strategies. Transfer learning has proven particularly effective in industrial applications, allowing rapid adaptation to new product lines with minimal training data requirements16."),17."). [...] However, traditional approaches face significant limitations. They require extensive manual feature engineering and parameter tuning, struggle with complex or varying defect patterns, and exhibit sensitivity to environmental conditions such as lighting variations. The preprocessing pipeline for the detection of steel plate surface defects exemplifies these challenges14."), requiring manual threshold setting and preset parameter configuration for the detection of the region-of-interest. Furthermore, traditional methods such as improved adaptive median filtering require boundary filling, grayscale judgment, and manual filter window size adjustment, which makes them computationally intensive and difficult to adapt to new defect types15.").

#

## Finding Common Ground

# With Peter DeWitt & Michael Nelson

A former K-5 public school principal turned author, presenter, and leadership coach, Peter DeWitt provides insights and advice for education leaders. Former superintendent Michael Nelson is a frequent contributor. Read more from this blog.

Artificial Intelligence  Opinion

# In the Race to Build Smarter AI, We Forgot to Build Smarter Humans

How schools can protect students’ cognition in the age of artificial intelligence

By Laura BaKosh — July 06, 2026   3 min read

 Save to favorites
 Print)

Email   Facebook   LinkedIn   Twitter   Copy URL

Laura BaKosh

Laura Bakosh, Ph.D., is the co-founder and CEO of Inner Explorer. [...] A Wharton field experiment with nearly 1,000 high school math students found the same trap. With AI tutors, students sailed through practice. When the tools were removed, those who had leaned on them scored 17 percent worse than peers who never used them. Most striking: They believed AI had helped them learn.

AI does not feel like it is hurting learning; it feels like progress. The erosion remains invisible until the tool is gone and the skill is gone. [...] ISTE spent the past three years testing and refining the tool, which is now ready for a wider audience.

Alyson Klein

items.title: AI Adoption Accelerating While Performance Stalls 06/29/2026 - MediaPost
items.summary:
Many of them have
created vibe-coded apps to solve problems. It's easy for anyone to be a vibe coder
items.content:
Many of them have
created vibe-coded apps to solve problems. It's easy for anyone to be a vibe coder, a software development practice assisted by AI where the software developer describes a project or task in a prompt
and the technology writes it.   
  
 "I think for many people, AI has turned them into a server of their ideas, or it has created forgeries of their ideas, because they have not had to do
the real work to determine if the idea will even work," Willis said, adding that companies look for that return in investments despite not being part of the original investment

When creating
an app in the past, it took an investment in resources and engineering time. They started with the problem, planned, and then prioritized. Then make a prototype and test. [...] by Laurie Sullivan
  , Staff Writer,
  June 29, 2026

Marketing technology leaders have
hoped artificial intelligence (AI) agents will alleviate some constraints, with 94% of high performers thinking the agentic technology will deliver significant operational efficiencies, and 98% think
it will deliver significant business performance results.

The reality remains that many organizations still struggle to see significant return on investment (ROI) from basic generative
AI applications, according to Gartner.

Chris Willis, chief design officer and futurist at Domo, pointed to some interesting data. AI's share of marketing activities has
nearly doubled in two years, from 13.1% to 24.2%, and generative AI has more than tripled, up 220%, from 7.0% to 22.4%.

advertisement

advertisement [...] advertisement

advertisement

Taking all this into consideration, the same survey notes that "not one marketing-technology activity scored above a 5 on a 7-point
performance scale, and those scores haven't moved in two years," he said. "It’s a plateau where adoption surged but results flatlined."

Willis said many companies Domo works with have
made "a lot of investments" in AI.

news

### This AI agent autonomously hacked a network, adapted on the fly, and demanded a ransom

By Gyana Swain

Jul 6, 20265 mins

Artificial IntelligenceRansomwareSecurity

opinion [...] News

Jul 6, 20265 mins

## JadePuffer exploited a vulnerable Langflow server, harvested credentials, moved laterally and encrypted more than 1,300 database records, marking what Sysdig describes as the first documented end-to-end agentic ransomware operation.

A fully autonomous AI agent conducted an end-to-end cyber intrusion and extortion campaign after exploiting a vulnerable Langflow server, demonstrating how large language models could accelerate ransomware operations, according to research published by Sysdig.

Sysdig detailed the operation in a research paper, saying the AI agent, dubbed JadePuffer, completed the entire intrusion chain, from initial access to database extortion, using an LLM to adapt its actions and execute more than 600 coordinated payloads. [...] “Modern EDR, XDR, and SOC platforms are built to flag malicious behavior rather than the underlying technology driving it,” Sharma said. “Whether an attack is carried out manually or orchestrated by an AI agent, actions such as credential abuse, privilege escalation, lateral movement, data exfiltration, and ransomware deployment still leave detectable behavioral traces.”

RansomwareSecurityArtificial Intelligence
